description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a slatted or grated appearance. The grooves are deep and extend the full width of the block, giving it a distinctive striped pattern when viewed from above. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a dark, matte finish that contrasts with the slightly lighter, textured top surface. The material appears to be a dense, dark stone, possibly granite or basalt, with a rough, unpolished texture. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a durable barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, giving it a striped, grated look. The sides are solid and dark, with a rough, unpolished stone texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the block's solid face and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, which run vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the same horizontal grooves visible on the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
